title: Coinbase
html:
Coinbase

The crypto exchange that anchored Yahir's late-2022 CCC-era pitch work — and one of the cleanest entity-level markers of the closed crypto chapter. Coinbase shows up in a single dense burst in 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream (the densest junk-drawer note in the corpus, written weeks after ChatGPT launched), and then vanishes from the surfaced corpus. It is therefore one of the most useful negative-space markers in the wiki: it shows where Yahir's attention was and where it is no longer.

The 2022 specimen reads as a structured project workstream. The to-do lines: "25% of coinbase financials", "Practice coinbase slides", "Finish coin base slides" — these are sequential, action-oriented entries suggesting Yahir was building a slide deck analyzing Coinbase's financials, likely for a OSIG (Oregon State Investment Group) presentation, a CCC meeting, or a class assignment in his OSU junior-year coursework. The percentage notation ("25% of") reads like a progress-tracker on the financials read. The "Practice" line implies a live presentation was scheduled.

The Coinbase work sits in the same note as the broader 2022 crypto cluster: the Oregon Blockchain Group event prep, the $PHIB token brainstorming ("how to explode PHIB / Twitter followers / mobile website / LOGOS!!!" — a memecoin he was apparently involved in promoting), the DAO mergers thesis, and the "join a DAO next cycle" line that explicitly framed crypto as cyclical. The combination — public-equity analysis of Coinbase + memecoin shilling + DAO speculation — is canonical late-2022 crypto-everything-everywhere. Yahir was running the mature (value investing applied to COIN equity) and immature ($PHIB) sides of the same market in parallel.

Per the first batch meta summary: "The 2022 Corvallis-Crypto-Club / Coinbase / DAO-mergers / $PHIB activity has zero echo in 2025-2026 notes. Yahir's attention moved fully to AI between 2023 and 2025." He honored the "join a DAO next cycle" read — when the AI cycle started, he switched cleanly. The Coinbase pitch deck is now a relic; the analytical muscle it built (read financials, build a deck, present a thesis) is the same muscle Rankett GTM bibles are built with three years later. Same skill, different category.
